item.title: Create docs/ROADMAP.md — what's planned, what's missing, what's prioritized
item.intent: Contributors and evaluators must see where the project is going — a project with no visible roadmap looks abandoned or directionless.
item.description: No roadmap document exists. The trajectory can be inferred from CHANGELOG.md (15 releases, v0.1.0 through v0.3.2, showing evolution from basic sprint runner to autonomous mode with grooming, validation, and diagnostics) and from known limitations (single-repo only, bash-only CLI, Claude Code dependency, no multi-language agent support, no remote/CI execution mode, no plugin system for custom agents). The roadmap should be organized as Now (active work) / Next (committed direction) / Later (vision) / Known Limitations (honest about current constraints). This is a lightweight document — 50-100 lines, not a product spec.
steps: Read CHANGELOG.md to understand the project trajectory and what has shipped (v0.1.0 through v0.3.2)
Review backlog/backlog.json and backlog/bugs.json for any remaining todo items that indicate planned work

Compose docs/ROADMAP.md with sections: Current Version (what v0.3.x delivers — summarize from CHANGELOG), Now (any active backlog items), Next (committed direction based on trajectory: what the next releases will focus on), Later (vision items: multi-repo support, CI/CD integration, plugin system, non-Claude agent support), Known Limitations (honest list: bash-only, single-repo, Claude Code dependency, macOS/Linux only)
Keep the document under 100 lines — this is a signpost, not a spec
Include a 'Want to help?' line at the bottom linking to docs/CONTRIBUTING.md
acceptanceCriteria: docs/ROADMAP.md exists with sections: Current Version, Now, Next, Later, Known Limitations
Known Limitations is honest about real constraints (bash-only, single-repo, Claude Code dependency)
Each roadmap item is a clear statement, not a vague aspiration
